slice: Phase 4 slice E - structural perf: effective-model memo above ModelBuilder; see maven-resolution-plan/phase4-results-e.md
harness: maven-resolution-plan/phase0-tools IntegratedBenchmark: MavenParser end-to-end, one JVM, ctx engine=legacy vs engine=maven over identical reactor inputs; warm iterations zero-download (RecordingHttpSender replay). This slice adds a PROFILE-LOOP line (phase attribution of the loopCtx steady state).

fix.E-1: Effective-model memo (MavenEngineResolution.buildEngineEffective): the mapped EngineEffective (ResolvedPom + effective Model + repositories/settings/reactor) is memoized ctx-lifetime (Caffeine, maximumSize 4096) keyed by (reactorEpoch | activeProfiles | requestedGav | xmlLength:xmlHash). The resolution facade and the dependency-graph facade each build the same module's effective pom (2 calls/module), and a steady-state re-resolution loop rebuilds every module every cycle; the memo serves all repeats. The global epoch (bumped by every UpdateMavenModel re-resolution) invalidates the whole memo on any reactor mutation, so a mutated parent is never served stale to an inheriting sibling.

notImplemented.item1-share-root-model-cache: Measured unnecessary on the available corpus. camel parents/BOMs are in-reactor (workspace-served, never model-cached regardless of store sharing); the descriptor path already shares session.getCache() (D-3); the memo already removes the per-module double root build. maven-15's one external super-parent (org.apache:apache) rebuilds ~15x/warm-parse but that is a tiny pom immeasurable against noise, and maven-15 already meets its target. Sharing the root store would require decoupling servedBy attribution from cache-freshness (a real correctness risk the parity fixtures guard) for no measurable corpus benefit.

environment.note: Absolute times differ from integrated-2026-07-09-post.json (a different host run); the meaningful comparison is the same-JVM within-tier ratio, re-measured here for both engines.

protocol.warm: fresh MavenPomCache and fresh ExecutionContext per iteration, zero network (replay); median - the honest first-parse cost
protocol.loopCtx: warm-cache re-resolution reusing ONE ExecutionContext (engine + DataPool + memo warm) - the true UpdateMavenModel steady state
protocol.note: legacy and maven within a tier are one JVM run (same-load ratio)

tiers.note: warm is noisy at 15 modules (absolute ~0.2s, 233-407 spread; a cleaner dedicated run read 213ms=1.42x). loopCtx 1.06x is the stable steady state. slice-D-post: 2.40x warm / 1.68x loop.
slice-D-post: 1.65x warm / 2.64x loop - the worst loop offender, now 1.04x. The loopProfile shows why: 0 model builds, all 800 buildEngineEffective calls served from the memo.

targets.loopGeLegacy: NEAR MET: loopCtx 1.04-1.10x (was 1.46-2.64x). Residual is the aether verbose collect (627-1011ms) that runs every re-resolution, which legacy mirrors.
targets.maven15Le15x: MET: loopCtx 1.06x steady state; warm 1.42x on a clean read (noisy at 15 modules).
targets.camel150WarmGeLegacy: NOT MET (no crossover): camel warm 1.26-1.49x. Floored by the per-descriptor DefaultModelBuilder cost - 400 root + 2803 descriptor effective-model builds per warm parse (residual #1 from slice D). Neither the memo nor a shared root model cache can close this; it is the inherent adapter cost of routing each descriptor through Maven's model builder.
